Walking soccer was a concept that Dr. Ben Drillings, who witnessed the sport being played outside of the city of Los Angeles, decided to bring it to Los Angeles . Dr. Drillings established a league for individuals age 60 and above with teams organized by nationality or ethnic background . This year, the Lanark walking soccer team represented Team USA in the Walking Soccer World Cup .

Dr. Ben Drillings created The Walking Soccer, a low-impact version of the game I love for players ages 60 to 85 . Dr. Drillings is a chiropractor and a former professional soccer player . Walking Soccer promotes exercise, balance and overall physical health . With the World Cup and the Olympics coming to Los Angeles, this is the perfect moment for our city to lead .

Don Leonardo Lopez is a leader that has strengthened our community's inspired generations, helping to make sure that we tell the very important story of our Latino heritage . With the love and partnership of his wife, Don Yairis, Don Leonardo went on to become a pioneer of many Latino-owned businesses . He founded the beloved restaurants such as El Mariachi, Leonardo's, and Labun .

Leonardo has created thousands of jobs, empowered entrepreneurs, and provided a platform for cultural celebration . Pico Rivera has served as the community and was even used as an evacuation and holding area for horses during the most recent January wildfires . It's a reflection his story is really a reflection of everything of what it means to be a Latino Everything that I believe is our Mexican cultural heritage .

He has been there for the city, for his family and for his community, for all the beautiful culture . Lopez: "It gives me such pride to say I'm a Mexican American and to see how you built this empire"

I'm proud of the movement that we've all created, our history. I was there in the Poor People's Campaign. I learned about the importance of black and brown unity . The 31st annual Festival of Philippine Arts and Culture will be held at Point Forman Park in San Pedro tomorrow . The festival was originally planned by the city's Department of Cultural Affairs back in 1990 . The first festival took place on Mother's Day, May 14th, 1992 at L.A. City College .

Massa Amini was arrested in Tehran by Iran's Morality Police for nothing more than the way she wore her hair . Her name has become a battle cry, not only in Iran but across the globe . Today, we speak her name to keep her spirit alive, to stand with the women of Iran and with women everywhere who face discrimination and violence . Let our memory be a call to action.

Robert O'Dell Knight Sr. was born in Soso, Mississippi in the 1930s . He was raised on a working farm where you learn to work when the Sun goes up and keep working until it goes down . He lived to the age of 89 years old leaving a remarkable legacy of faith dedication and community service .

Robert Knight was raised in a devout Christian family and church membership was a very important part of his life . Knight served honorably in the United States Army in the late 1950s where he assisted a flight surgeon between Costa Rica, Panama, and Columbia . Knight built houses throughout Southern California, single family homes, multi-unit apartments, housing developments, expanded his portfolio, but never became a corporate landlord .

Hushmand Areli was born in 1937 in the historic city of Isfahan in Iran . Hushman was studying with the great master Taj Esfahani, a revered figure in Iranian classical music . He helped stabilize neighborhoods, prevent displacement and provided affordable options for people throughout the region .

Hushman Darili was more than a vocalist, he was a messenger of memories . His music was filled with the essence of Persian poetry, emotion, and longing . Through his voice, generations found connection to their homeland, to their language, and to their roots .
